title: Coreopsis insularis
intro: Coreopsis insularis, also known as Island Coreopsis, is a flowering plant species native to the Hawaiian Islands, characterized by its bright yellow or orange daisy-like flowers. The plant has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, which can aid in treating wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Coreopsis insularis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and topical creams, with its dried flowers and leaves also being used in traditional Hawaiian remedies for their medicinal and spiritual value.
taxonomy: Coreopsis insularis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the Hawaiian Islands and is a member of the Coreopsis genus, which comprises annual and perennial plants commonly known as tickseed or calliopsis. The species Coreopsis insularis is further classified into the tribe Coreopsideae and the subfamily Asteroideae. The plant is also known as the Hawaiian tickseed or insular coreopsis, and is often cultivated for its bright yellow flowers. Coreopsis insularis is a relatively rare species due to habitat loss and other environmental factors.
uses: Coreopsis insularis has been studied for its potential medicinal properties, particularly in relation to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. However, more research is needed to fully understand its therapeutic applications. The plant's flowers and leaves have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and skin conditions. Some studies suggest that Coreopsis insularis may have antimicrobial properties, which could be beneficial in wound care and infection prevention. Further investigation is necessary to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Coreopsis insularis for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Coreopsis insularis is known for its potential health benefits due to its high content of antioxidants and flavonoids. It may help in reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Additionally, its antiseptic properties may aid in wound healing and infection prevention.
